message:
chore(beads): close bd-yf86e + bd-wkpcv

Both critical defects resolved in this branch:

- bd-yf86e (16da65d8) — .init_array deadlock from OnceLock self-recurse
  on HOST_CXA_THREAD_ATEXIT_IMPL. Every LD_PRELOAD-loaded binary hung at
  futex_wait before main(); the conformance gate scripts inherited the
  hang via setjmp_abi_test.

- bd-wkpcv (d503e6ce) — xdr_bytes / xdr_string / xdr_array /
  xdr_reference returned bump-arena pointers under parallel test
  pressure, then aborted glibc free with "free(): invalid size". Same
  family as bd-zgifl. The four allocate-on-decode + paired XDR_FREE
  sites now use libc::malloc / libc::free, matching the SunRPC
  mem_alloc/mem_free contract callers expect.

Sweep gate after both fixes: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --tests →
20 binaries, 999 tests, 0 failures, 0 SIGABRTs.

files.patch:
@@ -970,7 +970,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-wce6","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a invalid mode should return EAI_BADFLAGS with EINVAL before no-op success","description":"Host glibc rejects invalid getaddrinfo_a modes with EAI_BADFLAGS and errno=EINVAL even when the request set is degenerate (nitems=0 or all request slots NULL). FrankenLibC currently returns success because it short-circuits before validating mode. Validate mode first in unistd_abi.rs and add focused ABI regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:22.394510666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:41:59.924622294Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:41:59.922413963Z","close_reason":"Validated getaddrinfo_a mode before degenerate no-op success, matching host EAI_BADFLAGS/EINVAL behavior for invalid modes while preserving GAI_WAIT/GAI_NOWAIT success semantics on empty and all-NULL request sets; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wcot","title":"syscall: move_mount NULL source pathname with nonzero flags should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"Host glibc returns EPERM for move_mount(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, "/", 1) on this unprivileged host. Add ABI regression coverage so nonzero flags preserve the observed privilege-first result.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:13:44.623070986Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:31:56.140445735Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:31:56.138303389Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-wkpcv","title":"xdr_bytes/string/array/reference: raw_alloc-then-libc::free SIGABRT under parallel tests","description":"## Reproduction

```bash
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test \
  -p frankenlibc-abi --test xdr_abi_test
# free(): invalid size — SIGABRT
# Reproduces with --test-threads=2 or higher
# Single-threaded (-- --test-threads=1) passes 85/85
```

## Root cause (same family as bd-zgifl)

Tests in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/xdr_abi_test.rs` allocate via the
public XDR routines (`xdr_bytes` DECODE, `xdr_string` DECODE, `xdr_array`
DECODE, `xdr_reference` DECODE) and then free the returned buffer with
`libc::free` — which is the `mem_free` half of RFC 1832's
`mem_alloc`/`mem_free` contract.

The XDR routines were calling `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc`, which
delegates through `native_libc_malloc`. Under parallel test threads,
`NATIVE_MALLOC_REENTRY` (a process-global `AtomicBool` used as a
recursion guard) is observed `true` by a second thread that races the
first thread's host_malloc call. The CAS fails and the second thread
falls through to `bump_alloc`, which returns a static-arena pointer.

When the test then runs `libc::free(out_ptr)` on that bump pointer,
glibc's free aborts with `free(): invalid size` because the bump heap
has no glibc chunk metadata in front of it.

## Fix

Switch all four xdr decode-allocate sites and their paired XDR_FREE
sites in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs` from
`raw_alloc`/`raw_free` to `libc::malloc`/`libc::free` directly. Matches
the SunRPC `mem_alloc`/`mem_free` contract that callers (including the
tests and any C code linking against our libxdr surface) rely on.

Sites changed:
- `xdr_bytes` lines 1091/1105
- `xdr_string` lines 1151/1184
- `xdr_array` lines 1228/1251
- `xdr_reference` lines 1293/1307

## Validation

```
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test xdr_abi_test
# 85 passed; 0 failed (default thread count)
# 5/5 stress iterations pass

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test xdr_abi_test -- --test-threads=2
# 85 passed; 0 failed (was SIGABRT before fix)
```

## Severity

[CRITICAL] — every parallel `cargo test` run aborts with SIGABRT on this
test binary. Drains an entire ABI test surface from the supported gates.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:03:15.505285755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:03:15.505285755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-wtlg","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:51:44.009148022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:10:05.779454951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T19:10:05.776806248Z","close_reason":"Current tree already fixed: io_internal_abi uses stdio_abi tmpfile/fclose, replacement guard passes, and standalone cargo check succeeds","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wud","title":"Mode semantics matrix: strict vs hardened behavior table per API family","description":"Critique mapping: #2 + #5.

Deliverables:
- Machine-generated strict/hardened semantic delta table for each API family.
- Include which repairs are admissible and which are forbidden per mode.

Acceptance:
- Docs and harness consume same source table.
- No hidden mode-specific behavior without explicit table entry.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): Updated mode_semantics_matrix.json (Threading heals=0, Stdlib heals=10, added getenv/setenv/unsetenv). Fixed cascading test failures: replacement_profile.json (io_abi count 4->0, added stdlib_abi), verification_matrix.json (3 BrownCompass beads evidence + dashboard restructured), perf_budget_policy.json (230 symbols, getenv hotpath). All 213 harness tests green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:12.457005634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:46:04.548554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:46:04.548554Z","close_reason":"Mode semantics matrix updated with correct heals_call_sites counts, env stubs added, all 8 mode_semantics_test + 213 harness tests pass. Cascading fixes applied to replacement_profile.json (io_abi census=0, stdlib_abi added), verification_matrix.json (bd-182/bd-2bu/bd-3tc evidence), and perf_budget_policy.json.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wud","depends_on_id":"bd-2bu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1005,7 +1005,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ye0f4","title":"[CRITICAL] bd-g2bd5 regression: glibc_internal_abi_test still SIGABRTs on free(): invalid size under parallel test execution","description":"## Regression against bd-g2bd5

bd-g2bd5 was closed at 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z with close_reason 'Scoped __cxa_thread_atexit_impl capture to local TLS dtor tests and revalidated glibc/startup ABI suites' (commit a581f0ac 'br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations'). That fix is partial — the heap corruption still reproduces.

## Fresh repro at current HEAD (901b7bcc / 693bb766)

```
$ rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test --workspace --all-targets
...
test e2e_string_pipeline_strlen_malloc_memcpy_strcmp ... ok
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
  process didn't exit successfully: `.../glibc_internal_abi_test-bcae966c1a14117c` (signal: 6, SIGABRT: process abort signal)
```

Same binary re-run in isolation (`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`) now appears to pass — so the race has moved from 'reliably reproducing on isolated run' (pre-fix) to 'only reproducing when other workspace tests run concurrently in the same process pool' (post-fix). That's measurable progress, but the heap write onto a freed-or-wrong-size chunk is still live.

## Diagnosis

The a581f0ac fix scoped  capture to its test-local TLS dtor registrations. That closed **one** source of cross-test thread-local leakage. The SIGABRT still firing near  (in a different test binary) suggests either:

1. another set of test TLS/thread-local state is leaking through the same path (e.g. resolv-context override, IDNA allocation pool, wcstof128 static, obstack globals), OR
2. the fix captured TLS dtor registrations correctly but freed its scratch buffer via a path that is inconsistent with the allocation size metadata (e.g. Box::from_raw on a pointer allocated by a different allocator, or layout mismatch on dealloc).

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with MALLOC_CHECK_=2 to get a glibc backtrace from the first write past chunk-size metadata.
2. Rerun under ASan (RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' cargo +nightly test --workspace) — ASan will point at the exact offending free/realloc.
3. Audit remaining thread-local state init/teardown in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs plus any Box::from_raw / custom Drop impl that crosses allocators.

## Provenance / cross-reference

- Previous bead: bd-g2bd5 (closed 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z).
- Previous fix commit: a581f0ac br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations.
- Also closed this session: bd-i3om4 (70d3f3ea br-bd-i3om4: serialize runtime policy test state) and bd-nnjiz (901b7bcc br-bd-nnjiz: normalize elimination flake threshold). Both appear to hold under parallel reruns.

## Blast radius

Still blocks cargo test --workspace --all-targets from completing cleanly. --test-threads=1 is a workaround, not a fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:21:38.932129482Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:50:08.906939824Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:50:08.905039477Z","close_reason":"Fixed TLS-dtor test capture isolation; workspace now advances past glibc_internal_abi_test to pthread_abi_test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critical","full-review","glibc","heap","race","regression","test"]}
 {"id":"bd-yecy","title":"Execute ctype_ops fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade ctype_ops conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:24:38.190608519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T00:31:16.051680212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T00:31:16.048625797Z","close_reason":"Upgraded ctype_ops conformance coverage to execute every fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added top-level fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-yf86e","title":"ABI .init_array deadlock: HOST_CXA_THREAD_ATEXIT_IMPL OnceLock self-recurse","description":"## Reproduction

```bash
LIB=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so
cc -O2 tests/integration/fixture_setjmp_edges.c -o /tmp/fx
timeout 15 env LD_PRELOAD=$LIB /tmp/fx
# hangs forever; SIGTERM exits 124
```

`bash scripts/check_setjmp_native.sh` and `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test setjmp_abi_test` both inherit the hang.

## Diagnosis

strace shows the process stuck at `futex(<addr>, FUTEX_WAIT_BITSET_PRIVATE, 5, NULL, ...)` in libfrankenlibc_abi data segment, before ever reaching `main`. wchan=`futex_do_wait`, syscall=202.

Address resolution against `nm --numeric-sort` localizes the futex word to offset 8 of `HOST_CXA_THREAD_ATEXIT_IMPL` (a `std::sync::OnceLock<usize>` declared at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/startup_abi.rs:934). Futex value 5 = std `Once` RUNNING + has-waiter.

Cycle:
1. .init_array entry `frankenlibc_abi_stdio_init_entry` runs while ld.so still holds the loader lock.
2. Path eventually triggers `__cxa_thread_atexit_impl` (Rust TLS init for some thread_local with a non-trivial drop).
3. Our interposed `__cxa_thread_atexit_impl` calls `host_cxa_thread_atexit_impl()`.
4. That enters `OnceLock::get_or_init`, transitions state to RUNNING.
5. Closure invokes `dlfcn_abi::dlsym(RTLD_NEXT, "__cxa_thread_atexit_impl")`.
6. The dlsym path itself touches Rust TLS, triggering another `__cxa_thread_atexit_impl` registration.
7. Recursive call enters `OnceLock::get_or_init` again, sees RUNNING, futex-waits — on its own thread.

The existing `TLS_ATEXIT_REENTRY` guard only fires AFTER the host lookup, so it doesn't break this cycle.

## Fix (committed in same change as this bead)

Replace `OnceLock<usize>` with `AtomicUsize` (sentinel = `usize::MAX` for resolved-but-absent) plus a thread-local reentry guard that returns `None` if the resolver re-enters us mid-lookup. AtomicUsize never blocks, so recursive lookups simply re-resolve or fall through to the in-Rust fallback list. Mirrors the proven pattern already used by `host_resolve.rs`.

## Validation

- `LD_PRELOAD=…/libfrankenlibc_abi.so /tmp/fx` → "fixture_setjmp_edges: PASS" in <1s
- `bash scripts/check_setjmp_native.sh` → "check_setjmp_native: PASS"

## Severity

[CRITICAL] — every LD_PRELOAD-loaded binary hangs at process start under release profile. Affects all conformance scripts that go through .init_array bootstrap.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T23:58:33.742496547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:58:33.742496547Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Address resolution against `nm --numeric-sort` localizes the futex word to offset 8 of `HOST_CXA_THREAD_ATEXIT_IMPL` (a `std::sync::OnceLock<usize>` declared at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/startup_abi.rs:934). Futex value 5 = std `Once` RUNNING + has-waiter.

4. That enters `OnceLock::get_or_init`, transitions state to RUNNING.

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the null-mask contract is frozen exactly when sizemask is otherwise valid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.404095392Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:23:38.031316789Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:23:38.029099401Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity signalfd4(-1, NULL, 8, 0) behavior: exact EFAULT and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-yise","title":"syscall: execute termios_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/termios_ops_conformance_test.rs only checks termios fixture metadata even though the shared conformance executor already implements tcgetattr/cfgetispeed/cfgetospeed/cfsetispeed/cfsetospeed cases. Add harness-matrix execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/termios_ops.json, assert expected_output/host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any termios parity mismatches the executable coverage surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:00:17.045227069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:20:06.523870985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:20:06.521529243Z","close_reason":"Executed termios_ops fixtures through the harness matrix and normalized tcgetattr placeholder output to match the fixture contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
